These portals persist until the end of her next turn, and creatures of any size can step move through either, using 5 feet of movement to exit from the other portal." special="At the end of her next turn, she can spend an additional 2 Portal Points as a @HLfree_action to extend the portals' duration by 1 round." replace="Poison immunity"}

profiler.name: Profiler
description: ## Profiler
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/55›
Profilers understand the human psyche to an intense degree, allowing them to predict the criminals they hunt.

::ab[Expert Profiler (Ex)]{icon=boost-power l1="A profiler gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his class level (minimum +1) on Sense Motive checks." l2="He can use *inspiration* on any Sense Motive check without expending a use of *inspiration,* provided he is trained in the skill." l3="He can attempt to predict a creature's movements using his knowledge of the creature's psychology. He can use this ability only on a creature familiar to him (for example, a ‹arc-vigilante/serial killer› whose crimes the profiler has studied will suffice, even if the profiler hasn't yet identified the serial killer's true identity). To do so, he starts from a place where he knows that the creature has been and why (most often the scene of a crime) and attempts a Sense Motive check against a DC equal to 40 or to 20 + the creature's Bluff modifier, whichever is higher, which the GM rolls in secret. On a failure, the profiler's guess about the creatures movements is inaccurate. On a success, the profiler is able to track the creature's general movements as if using Survival to track (though the usual modifiers on Survival checks to track don't apply, and this ability allows her to track a creature using pass without trace or otherwise leaving no trail). After at most 1 hour (or sooner if there are many possible options for the creature to take, at the GM's discretion), if the profiler has not yet found further evidence of the creature's passing (such as footprints, fingerprints, or any other indication that the creature came this way), he loses track of the creature's movements; if he does, he can try to guess where the creature went next by attempting another check." replace="Trapfinding~poison lore~trap sense"}

::ab[Divination Analysis (Su)]{l=2 icon=boost-def ability="A profiler has analyzed the workings of the mind. His caster level to determine the duration of his divination extracts increases by 1, and he gains a +1 bonus on saving throws against divinations." imp5="The increases to caster level becomes 2 and the bonus on saving throws increases to +2." imp8="These increase to 3 and +3, respectively." imp11="A profiler can expend one use of *inspiration* to add 1d6 to the result of a ‹rule/concentration› check when using a divination extract or investigator spell-like ability." replace="Poison resistance~poison immunity"}

::ab[Blood Sleuth (Sp)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="A profiler can expend one use of his *inspiration* to use ‹spell/discern next of kin› as a spell-like ability. Alternatively, he can expend two uses of *inspiration* to use ‹spell/blood biography› as a spell-like ability." replace="Swift alchemy"}

::ab[Pack Psychology (Su)]{l=7 icon=def passive="A profiler can understand the way that groups act together, allowing him to exploit the vulnerabilities in their attacks. A profiler can't be ‹rule/flanked› by creatures with an Intelligence score and he gains a +1 bonus to his AC against such creatures when they would otherwise flank him, as he plays their movements against each other." replace="Investigator talent at 7th level"}

psychic_detective.name: Psychic Detective
description: ## Psychic Detective
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/119›
A psychic detective supplements her keen insight with occult skill to unravel mysteries both ordinary and supernatural.
::cskill{lose=acro~climb~perf}
:::ab[Spells]{icon=magic title=Spells action=Ability}

A psychic detective casts psychic spells drawn from the ‹main/spells_>psychic« class spell list› and augmented by a select set of additional spells (see *Psychic Detective Spells* below). Only spells from the psychic class spell list of 6th level or lower and psychic detective spells are considered to be part of the psychic detective's spell list. If a spell appears on both the psychic detective and psychic class spell lists, the psychic detective uses the spell level from the psychic detective spell list. She can cast any spell she knows without preparing it ahead of time. To learn or cast a spell, a psychic detective must have an Intelligence score equal to at least 10 + the spell's level. The saving throw DC against a psychic detective's spell is 10 + the spell's level + the psychic detective's Intelligence modifier.

Like other spellcasters, a psychic detective can cast only a certain number of spells of each spell level per day. She knows the same number of spells and receives the same number of spells slots per day as a ‹class/bard› of her investigator level, and knows and uses 0-level knacks as a bard uses cantrips. In addition, she receives ‹rule/bonus spells« per day› if she has a high Intelligence score.

At 5th level and every 3 levels thereafter, a psychic detective can choose to learn a new spell in place of one she already knows, using the same rules as a bard. In effect, the psychic detective loses the old spell in exchange for the new one. The new spell's level must be the same as that of the spell being exchanged, and it must be at least 1 level lower than the highest-level spell the psychic detective can cast.

A psychic detective need not prepare her spells in advance. She can cast any psychic detective spell she knows at any time, assuming she has not yet used up her allotment of spells per day for the spell's level.

::ab[Psychic Detective Spells]{icon=learn note="A psychic detective has the following spells on her spell list, in addition to psychic spells of 6th level or lower." s1="Detect chaos~~detect evil~~detect good~~detect law~~find traps" s3="Calm spirit~~daylight" s4="Modify memory" s6="Banishment~~moment of prescience~~greater scrying" replace=Alchemy}

::ab[Psychic Meddler (Su)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A psychic detective receives a +1 bonus on saves against psychic spells and spell-like abilities." increment="This bonus~2~3" imp5="In addition, the psychic investigator now adds ‹misc/half› this bonus on caster level checks to dispel psychic effects." replace="Poison lore~poison resistance~swift alchemy~poison immunity"}

::ab[Phrenic Dabbler (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A psychic detective gains a small pool of phrenic points equal to 1/2 her psychic detective level, as well as one ‹ability/phrenic amplification<s›, as the ‹class/psychic› class feature. This does not allow the psychic detective to qualify for the ‹feat/Extra Amplification› feat." replace="Investigator talent gained at 3rd level"}

::ab[Investigator Talents]{icon=power ability="A psychic detective selects investigator talents as normal, except she cannot select the ‹invtalent/alchemist discovery› investigator talent. She can select the ‹feat/Expanded Phrenic Pool› feat as a talent even though she doesn't meet that feat's prerequisites, and she can select an extra phrenic amplification once as a talent." alter="Investigator talents"}

@HL[Investigator Talents:] The following investigator talents complement the psychic detective archetype: ‹invtalent/confusing strike›, ‹invtalent/device talent›, ‹invtalent/effortless aid›, ‹invtalent/hidden agendas›, ‹invtalent/inspired intelligence›, ‹invtalent/item lore›, and ‹invtalent/unconventional inspiration›.

questioner.name: Questioner
description: ## Questioner
‹SOURCE Magic Tactics Toolbox/26›

Dabblers in arcane magic and masters of stealth and guile, questioners are investigators who often find themselves mucking about in cases for less-than-savory clientele or that require an extra bit of subtlety. Although questioners are found in nearly every major city in the Inner Sea region, Ustalav has the highest concentration; many belong to the famed Sleepless Detectives, while others have trained to evade the notice of that acclaimed agency.

::ab[Inspiration for Subterfuge (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A questioner can use *inspiration* on any Knowledge, Linguistics, or Stealth checks he attempts without expending a use of *inspiration,* provided he's trained in that skill." alter=Inspiration}
:::ab[Spells]{icon=magic title=Spells action=Ability replace=Alchemy}

A questioner casts arcane spells drawn from the ‹main/bard spell« list›. He can cast any spell he knows without preparing it ahead of time. To learn or cast a spell, a questioner must have an Intelligence score equal to at least 10 + the spell's level. The saving throw DC against a questioner's spell is equal to 10 + the spell's level + the questioner's Intelligence modifier.

Like other spellcasters, a questioner can cast only a certain number of spells of each spell level per day. He knows the same number of spells and receives the same number of spell slots per day as a ‹class/bard› of his investigator level, including for cantrips. In addition, he receives ‹rule/bonus spells« per day› if he has a high Intelligence score.

At 5th level and every 3 investigator levels thereafter, a questioner can learn a new spell in place of one he already knows, using the same rules as a bard. In effect, the questioner loses the old spell in exchange for the new one. The new spell's level must be the same as that of the spell being exchanged, and it must be at least 1 level lower than the highest-level spell the questioner can cast.
A questioner need not prepare his spells in advance. He can cast any bard spell he knows at any time, assuming he has not yet used up his allotment of spells per day for the spell's level.

::ab[Know-It-All (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A questioner receives a +1 bonus on skill checks for all Knowledge skills in which he is trained." special="If he has the ‹invtalent/perceptive tracking› investigator talent, he also adds this bonus on Perception checks to find and follow tracks." imp5="Also, the questioner gains the ‹invtalent/eidetic recollection› investigator talent as a bonus talent, ignoring its prerequisites." replace="Poison lore" increment="This bonus~2~3"}

::ab[Restricted Talents]{icon=down note="A questioner selects ‹ability/investigator talents› as normal, except he cannot select the ‹invtalent/alchemist discovery› investigator talent." alter="Investigator talents"}

@HL[Investigator Talents:] The following investigator talents complement the questioner archetype particularly well: ‹invtalent/expanded inspiration›, ‹invtalent/inspirational expertise›, *perceptive tracking,* ‹invtalent/rogue talent›, and ‹invtalent/underworld inspiration›.

reckless_epicurean.name: Reckless Epicurean
description: ## Reckless Epicurean
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/14›

Reckless epicureans are investigators who, for whatever reason, tend to test unknown concoctions on themselves. Whether an obsessive scientist, a healer determined to ensure the safety of her tinctures, or a seeker of new and interesting potions, a reckless epicurean's body is saturated with experimental chemicals and magic. She can prepare extracts without formulae by approximating the taste, and she can guzzle cursed concoctions without batting an eyelash.

::ab[A Familiar Taste (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A reckless epicurean adds half her level as a bonus on Perception and Spellcraft checks to identify potions. The benefits she receives from *poison resistance* and *poison immunity* also apply against harmful potions and other ingested substances with negative effects, such as drugs." replace=Trapfinding alter="Poison resistance~poison immunity"}

::ab[Experimental Potable (Su)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="A reckless epicurean can prepare one *experimental potable* using a formula that she does not know from the alchemist formulae list into an extract slot of the appropriate level. These *experimental potables* often have side effects. When a creature consumes one of these potables, the investigator must succeed at a Spellcraft check (DC = 15 + the formula's level). On a failure, she must roll on the ‹rule/Wild Magic« Surge table› to determine the extra effects. If the wild magic surge effect isn't possible due to the nature of the spell or effect, roll instead on the Universal Surge Effects table. Results concerning the caster affect the drinker of the *experimental potable* instead." incrementPlain="She can now~5~3" incrementDesc=prepare~create incrementEnd=" *experimental potables* per day." replace="Investigator talent gained at 5th level"}

::ab[Sympathetic Resistance (Su)]{l=13 icon=def passive="When a reckless epicurean drinks an extract or a potion, she gains an alchemical bonus on saves against spells and spell-like abilities of the same school as that potion or extract for a number of rounds equal to her Intelligence modifier. This bonus is equal to the level of the spell contained in the extract or potion." useMod=Intelligence useM replace="Investigator talent gained at 13th level"}

ruthless_agent.name: Ruthless Agent
description: ## Ruthless Agent
‹SOURCE Antihero's Handbook/27›

Ruthless agents are often called upon for their skills at extracting information by any means necessary. Though some view their methods as extreme, ruthless agents prize getting the job done at all costs. Ruthless agents can be found in the employ of nearly any organization - even goodly groups whose leaders either don't know the extent of ruthless agents' zeal or are willing to look the other way to take advantage of these investigators' incredible efficiency.
::prereq{other="Alignment must be nongood."}

::ab[Inspiration (Ex)]{icon=power-down flavor="A ruthless agent focuses on reading motivations and using interrogation tactics." ability="She can use *inspiration* on Intimidate, Knowledge, and Sense Motive checks without expending a use of *inspiration,* provided she's trained in the skill. A ruthless agent can't spend *inspiration* on attack rolls or saving throws." alter=Inspiration}

::ab[Enhanced Intimidation (Ex)]{icon=boost order=pa passive="A ruthless agent adds half her investigator level (minimum +1) as a bonus on ‹skill/Intimidate› checks. When the ruthless agent uses Intimidate to influence an opponent's attitude, the duration of the attitude shift is 1d6&times&10 minutes per investigator level (rather than the normal 1d6&times&10 minutes)." ability="Additionally, the ruthless agent is an expert in blending coercion and fear. The first time the ruthless agent attempts to use Intimidate to influence an opponent's attitude and fails, she can retry it against that same opponent with no increase to the DC. Subsequent attempts to influence the same opponent are subject to increased DCs as normal." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Interrogate (Su)]{l=3 icon=lower fullround="A ruthless agent can ask a target one question for every 2 investigator levels she has. The target is not compelled to answer truthfully, but the ruthless agent's implicit or explicit threats and looming manner leads the target to believe the ruthless agent will cause it serious harm if it lies. This imposes a -2 penalty on the target's Bluff checks to lie when answering the specific questions the ruthless agent asked." increment="The penalty~3~3~-4~-2" useMod=Intelligence3 special="This is a mind-affecting fear effect." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Agonizing Strike (Ex)]{l=4 icon=lower flavor="A ruthless agent has learned how to finesse her attacks to discomfort a foe." ability="When she deals damage using her *studied strike,* she can make this damage nonlethal (regardless of whether her weapon normally deals lethal or nonlethal damage), forcing the target to succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + half the ruthless agent's level + her Intelligence modifier) or be ‹misc/sickened› for 1 round and take a -2 penalty on Will saving throws for 1 minute." special="Multiple agonizing strikes do not worsen the condition or impose an additional penalty on Will saves, but they do increase the duration of both effects." replace="Swift alchemy"}

::ab[Concoction of Truth (Su)]{l=7 icon=learn note="A ruthless agent automatically adds ‹spell/discern lies› to her list of formulae known. She prepares this as a 3rd-level extract, and she can prepare one *discern lies* extract each day that does not count against her daily allotment of extracts." replace="Investigator talent gained at 7th level"}

::ab[Compel Obedience (Sp)]{l=11 icon=magic fullround="A ruthless agent can issue a binding command to a creature once per day. This command acts like ‹spell/geas/quest› with the creature as the target, using the ruthless agent's investigator level as the caster level. The ruthless agent can compel obedience from only one creature at a time (as soon as the *geas/quest* is fulfilled, however, the ruthless agent can use this ability again, as long as she has a use of this ability available)." imp17="The ruthless agent can compel obedience from two creatures at once, and she is always aware of the targets as if they were under the effects of a ‹spell/status› spell (caster level = the ruthless agent's investigator level)." replace="Investigator talents gained at 11th and 17th levels"}

scavenger.name: Scavenger
description: ## Scavenger
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/21›

Scavengers are masters of systems: how they fit together, why they work, and how to make the most of their parts. However, scavengers generally lack other investigative talents such as social skills or an understanding of history and lore. The ratfolk of Numeria excel at this art, but nearly every warren across Golarion boasts at least a few resourceful scavengers.
::prereq{r=Ratfolk}
:::ab{title="Gadgetry (Su)" action=Ability icon=boost-power alter=Alchemy}

A scavenger is a master of mechanical arts rather than alchemical ones, and he gains a competence bonus on Craft (clockwork) checks equal to his class level. He can use Knowledge (engineering) to identify wondrous items as if using ‹spell/detect magic› and Spellcraft. A scavenger does not gain any bonuses to or special uses of the Craft (alchemy) skill.

Rather than prepare his extracts as consumable liquids, a scavenger constructs small, single-use devices infused with bits of his own magic aura to achieve the same effects. Alchemist and investigator discoveries that affect extracts result in identical effects on a scavenger's gadgets (such as the ‹discovery/infusion› discovery, which would allow another creature to use a gadget, just as it would allow that creature to use an extract).

::ab[Mechanical Inspiration (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power-down ability="A scavenger can use *inspiration* to apply a bonus to Appraise, Disable Device, and Knowledge (engineering) checks without expending a use of *inspiration,* but he must spend a use of *inspiration* to apply a bonus on Knowledge, Linguistics, and Spellcraft checks." alter=Inspiration}

::ab[Jury-Rig (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A scavenger can sacrifice one of his gadgets to repair or enhance an adjacent mechanical device, such as a lock, trap, or vehicle. Doing so either repairs 1d6 points of damage per extract level of the gadget sacrificed or applies a bonus or penalty to one specific skill check involved in using the device. The scavenger could, for example, sacrifice a gadget to impose a penalty on all Disable Device checks to disarm a trap, add a bonus on all Disable Device checks to open a lock, or add a bonus on all Profession (driver) checks to drive a carriage. This modifier is equal to twice the extract level of the gadget sacrificed, and lasts for 10 minutes per class level." replace="Poison lore"}

::ab[Construct Mastery (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A scavenger gains a +2 bonus on all Craft checks to modify or repair creatures of the construct type, as well as a +2 bonus on all weapon damage rolls against constructs." imp5="These bonuses become +4." imp8="These bonuses increase to +6." imp11="The scavenger gains ‹feat/Craft Construct› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites." replace="Poison resistance~poison immunity"}

skeptic.name: Skeptic
description: ## Skeptic
‹SOURCE Haunted Heroes Handbook/11›

The skeptic accepts the existence of the occult world while challenging claims that the supernatural explains all of life's problems. Skeptics tirelessly strive to debunk the legends surrounding possessions and spiritual hauntings, but stand prepared on the off chance a legend turns out to be true. Though these strong-willed investigators master the art of identifying and overcoming haunts and possessions, they often revel in the thrill of revealing a more mundane truth.

::ab[Hauntfinding (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost flavor="A skeptic's knowledge of ‹rule/haunts› allows her to notice them with ease, regardless of the methods by which the haunts make their presence known in the realm of the living." passive="The skeptic adds 1/2 her level to all skill checks to notice haunts. If she notices a haunt, she adds this same bonus to any initiative check made as a result of that haunt activating. This bonus is an insight bonus." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Suspect Hoax (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="Regardless of the actual situation at hand, a skeptic naturally suspects that trickery or hoaxing is at hand. This mindset helps to set the skeptic at ease even when confronted by the most terrifying of situations, be the source of that fear mundane or legitimately supernatural." passive="A skeptic gains a +1 insight bonus on saving throws against spells and spell-like abilities used to falsely create the impression of a supernatural presence, as well as a +1 insight bonus on saving throws caused by the effects of actual haunts or incorporeal undead." imp5="This bonus increases to +2." imp8="The bonus becomes +3." imp11="A skeptic becomes immune to fear effects." replace="Poison resistance"}

::ab[Smite Haunt (Su)]{flavor="Normally, damaging a haunt is a tricky affair requiring the ability to channel positive energy or wield spells capable of doing the same, but the skeptic learns methods of using her own convictions to accomplish the same feat." l=4 icon=zap standard="A skeptic can focus her willpower to damage an active haunt. She must be within a haunt's area to smite the haunt. When the skeptic uses this ability, she deals an amount of damage to the haunt equal to her *studied strike* bonus." replace="Swift alchemy"}

::ab[Exorcising Touch (Su)]{l=7 icon=touch flavor="A skeptic can damage a creature currently possessing another without lethally harming the possessed creature." ability="To make an exorcising strike, the skeptic must make a touch attack against the possessed creature. On a hit, she damages the possessing creature or entity, dealing an amount of lethal damage equal to her *studied strike* bonus damage. The possessed creature takes this amount of damage as nonlethal damage at the same time." replace="Investigator talent gained at 7th level"}

sleuth.name: Sleuth
description: ## Sleuth
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/101›

A sleuth is an investigator who relies on good fortune and guile rather than alchemy. Having no mystical energy intrinsic to her, she must forgo the more magical aspects of alchemy to solve her mysteries with wits, gumption, and the fickle consideration of luck.

:::ab{title="Sleuth's Luck (Ex)" l=1 icon=power-down action=Ability special="A sleuth cannot take the ‹invtalent/alchemist discovery› talent or any investigator talent that affects alchemy." replace=Alchemy}

A sleuth gains a fluctuating pool of luck, measuring her ability to get out of scrapes. At the start of each day, a sleuth has a number of luck points equal to her Charisma modifier (minimum 1). Her luck goes up or down throughout the day, but usually cannot go higher than her Charisma modifier (minimum 1), though feats, magic items, and spells that grant either *grit* or *panache* points can also grant a sleuth luck points equal to the amount of *grit* or *panache* they grant. A sleuth spends luck to accomplish *deeds* (see below), and regains luck in the following ways.

@HL[Rolling a Natural 20 on a Knowledge or Sense Motive Check:] While on an investigation, a sleuth regains luck by uncovering secrets. Rolling a natural 20 on these skill checks while actively investigating causes the sleuth to regain 1 luck point. Such skill checks made for more mundane reasons (such as normal research or using Sense Motive to gain information during normal bargaining or while gambling) do not regain luck for the sleuth. Determining which skill checks qualify is up to the GM.

@HL[Rolling a 6 or Higher on an Inspiration Roll:] When a sleuth rolls an inspiration die while on an investigation and the die comes up a 6 or higher, she regains 1 luck point. (If she rolls multiple inspiration dice at a time, she regains 1 luck point if the total is 6 or higher.) Like regaining luck via a natural 20 on a qualifying skill check, *inspiration* checks made for more mundane reasons don't qualify for regaining luck. The GM is the final arbitrator for what rolls qualify." special="It's possible for an investigator to regain 2 luck points on the same Skill check, if both the qualifying skill check is a natural 20 and any *inspiration* roll made is a 6 or higher.

::ab[Deeds]{head l=1 icon=power ability="Sleuths spend luck points to accomplish deeds. Most deeds grant a sleuth some momentary bonus or effect, but there are some that provide longer-lasting effects. Some deeds stay in effect as long as a sleuth has at least 1 luck point." replace="Swift alchemy"}

::ab[Daring (Ex)]{next icon=boost-roll ability="A sleuth can spend 1 luck point when he makes an Acrobatics, Climb, Escape Artist, Fly, Ride, or Swim check to roll d6 and add the result to the check. He can choose to add this die after he rolls. If the d6 roll is a natural 6, he rolls another d6 and adds it to the check. He can continue to do this as long as he rolls natural 6s, up to a number of times equal to his Intelligence modifier (minimum 1)."}

::ab[Opportunistic Evasion (Ex)]{next icon=def immediate="When a sleuth succeeds at a Reflex saving throw against an effect that still deals damage on a successful save, he can spend 1 luck point to instead take no damage for that effect."}

::ab[Sleuth's Initiative (Ex)]{next icon=boost-power passive="As long as the sleuth has at least 1 luck point, he gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks." order=pa ability="If he has the ‹feat/Quick Draw› feat, his hands are free and unrestrained, and the weapon is not hidden, he can draw a single light or one-handed melee weapon as part of the initiative check. (Unlike with swashbuckler deeds, this does not have to be a piercing weapon.)"}

::ab[Make It Count (Ex)]{prereq="4th-level investigator" icon=power next ability="When a sleuth uses *studied strike,* he can spend 1 luck point to apply an investigator talent that he doesn't already know and which affects *studied strike* to his *studied strike.* He must be able to fulfill that talent's prerequisites."}

::ab[Run Like Hell (Ex)]{next prereq="4th-level investigator" icon=boost ability="A sleuth can spend 1 luck point to gain a +20-foot bonus to his speed for 1 minute. Furthermore, while under the effect of this bonus, if he moves more than his normal speed in a round, he gains a +4 bonus to AC until the start of his next turn."}

::ab[Second Chance (Ex)]{l=4 icon=roll ability="When a sleuth rolls an inspiration die or uses the *daring* deed, he can spend 1 luck point to reroll either the *inspiration* or the *daring* deed die. If he rolls a 6 or higher on this reroll, he does not regain a luck point, and no matter what he rolls on either roll, he must keep the reroll result, even if it is lower."}

@HL[Investigator Talents:] The following investigator talents complement the sleuth archetype: ‹invtalent/amazing inspiration›, ‹invtalent/combat inspiration›, ‹invtalent/device talent›, ‹invtalent/effortless aid›, ‹invtalent/expanded inspiration›, ‹invtalent/hidden agendas›, ‹invtalent/inspirational expertise›, ‹invtalent/inspired alertness›, ‹invtalent/inspired intimidator›, ‹invtalent/perceptive tracking›, ‹invtalent/quick study›, ‹invtalent/rogue talent›, ‹invtalent/tenacious inspiration›, and ‹invtalent/underworld inspiration›.
### Grit, Luck, and Panache

[Grit](class/gunslinger), luck, and [panache](class/swashbuckler) represent three different means by which heroes can gain access to the same heroic pool, using it to accomplish fantastic feats. For characters with a mix of *grit, luck,* and *panache,* they pool the three resources together into a combined pool. (Those who use *panache* and *luck* do not gain twice their Charisma bonus in their pool.) For feats, magic items, and other effects, a *panache* user can spend and gain *luck* points in place of *grit* or *panache* points, and vice versa.
A luck user does not count as a *grit* or *panache* user to satisfy feat prerequisites.

spiritualist.name: Spiritualist
description: ## Spiritualist
>***Note:** This is an investigator archetype, and has no relation to the ‹class/spiritualist› occult class.*
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/102›

While most investigators look to the physical world to gain their knowledge, there are those who seek out knowledge beyond the pale. Those who think that the dead tell no tales are quickly proven wrong by the spiritualist. Instead of toying with chemicals and reagents to find clues, he talks directly to the spirit world to uncover the ways and means of skullduggery and the desperate acts committed in the heat of dark passions.

::ab[Commune with Spirits (Sp)]{l=1 icon=magic-down flavor="Instead of relying on alchemical research to find clues, a spiritualist relies on communion with the world beyond death." ability="A spiritualist gains the ability to use ‹spell/comprehend languages›, ‹spell/detect secret doors›, and ‹spell/identify›, but each has a casting time of 1 minute, as the spiritualist needs to consult with the spirits of the dead that dwell in his area or that are somehow attached to him in some way." usage="A number of times per day equal to his investigator level + his Wisdom modifier." order=uan note="As he goes up in level, the spiritualist gains the ability to use other spells with the casting time as above. Each of these spells requires one use of this ability." imp5="He can use ‹spell/augury›." imp7="He can use ‹spell/speak with dead› and ‹spell/locate object›." imp9="He can use ‹spell/legend lore›." replace=Alchemy special="A spiritualist cannot take the ‹invtalent/alchemist discovery› talent or any investigator talent that affects alchemy."}

::ab[Spirit Sense (Su)]{l=2 icon=def ability="When a spiritualist attempts a saving throw against an ability or an effect delivered by an incorporeal creature, he can expend one use of his *inspiration* instead of two to augment that saving throw. If the spiritualist has the ‹invtalent/combat inspiration› investigator talent, he need not spend any *inspiration* to augment such saving throws." replace="Poison lore"}

::ab[Strong Life (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="His dealings with the other side and knowledge of what lies beyond strengthens the spiritualist's will." passive="He gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against death effects and negative energy damage." imp5="This bonus increases to +4." imp8="This bonus becomes +6." replace="Poison resistance"}

::ab[Sixth Sense (Su)]{l=3 icon=def-roll ability="A spiritualist can spend one of his uses of the *commune with spirits* ability to reroll a single saving throw that he has failed. He must take the results of the reroll, even if it is lower." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Whispering Spirits (Su)]{l=4 icon=def flavor="The spiritualist can open a conduit with the spirit world that can aid him in combat for a short time." ability="He can expend one use of his *commune with spirits* ability in order to gain an insight bonus to both AC and saving throws equal to his Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) for 1 minute." replace="Swift alchemy"}

::ab[Touched by the Beyond (Su)]{l=11 icon=def flavor="The spiritualist's ability to touch the beyond grants him further protection against the dangers of death and negative energy." passive="The spiritualist becomes immune to death effects, and he takes half damage from negative energy." replace="Poison immunity"}

@HL[Investigator Talents:] The following investigator talents complement the spiritualist archetype: ‹invtalent/amazing inspiration›, ‹invtalent/combat inspiration›, ‹invtalent/effortless aid›, ‹invtalent/eidetic recollection›, ‹invtalent/empathy›, ‹invtalent/expanded inspiration›, ‹invtalent/hidden agendas›, ‹invtalent/inspirational expertise›, ‹invtalent/item lore›, ‹invtalent/studied defense›, and ‹invtalent/tenacious inspiration›.

star_watcher.name: Star Watcher
description: ## Star Watcher
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/57›

Star watchers study the movements and positions of the sun, the moon, and the stars, searching for secrets written in the sky. They use a combination of scientific study and esoteric arts to measure, record, and predict astronomical cycles and their effects on nature, such as the moon's influence on tides and rivers. Some star watchers also use these observations to predict fate and significant events, such as natural disasters.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down ability="A star watcher gains proficiency with the ‹eq-weapon/starknife›, but not with the rapier." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

:::ab{title="Astrology (Su)" action=Ability icon=magic-down alter=Alchemy special="A star watcher doesn't gain a competence bonus on Craft (alchemy) checks to create alchemical items, and he cannot identify potions with that skill. Furthermore, a star watcher cannot select alchemist discoveries as investigator talents. A star watcher can study a ‹class/wizard«'s› spellbook or ‹class/alchemist«'s› formula book to learn formulae, but a star watcher's formula book is too esoteric for anyone except another star watcher to learn spells from."}

A star watcher studies a symbolic language of the heavens known as *astrology.* Equal parts art form and science, *astrology* can be used to read the future and affect it in subtle ways. Much like an alchemist or investigator, a star watcher collects formulae in a formula book. However, the formulae resemble star charts, and unlike alchemists or normal investigators, he uses these formulae along with personal details and astronomical measurements to make horoscopes - magical calculations stored much like scrolls - in which he can store spells. In many ways, the star watcher's horoscopes function like extracts, with the following exceptions.

A star watcher's horoscope is always keyed to a specific creature that must be willing and present when the star watcher prepares the horoscope. Only the creature to which a horoscope is keyed can activate it and be affected by it. A horoscope is "cast" by reading it as a @HL[standard action], though a star watcher can draw and read a horoscope as a single @HL[standard action].

::ab[Starfinding (Ex)]{icon=boost-power ability="A star watcher adds half his level (minimum 1) as a bonus on Knowledge (geography) checks. He can also use astrological methods to read people. By checking his star charts, he can use Knowledge (geography) in place of Sense Motive checks." replace="Trapfinding~trap sense"}

::ab[Investigator Talents]{head icon=power ability="A star watcher can select the following talents, in addition to general ‹ability/investigator talents›." replace="Swift alchemy" alter="Investigator talents"}

::ab[Horoscopic Scrolls (Su)]{next icon=power ability="The star watcher gains ‹feat/Scribe Scroll› as a bonus feat. He can use the feat to create an arcane scroll of any formula he knows. Unlike horoscopes created with the *astrology* ability, anyone capable of using arcane scrolls can cast a spell from these scrolls. Additionally, the star watcher can use scrolls as if he were an arcane spellcaster. He can use Knowledge (geography) to identify scrolls as if using ‹spell/detect magic›. He must hold the scroll for 1 round to attempt such a check."}

::ab[Precognition (Su)]{prereq="4th-level investigator" next icon=magic flavor="A star watcher can glimpse into the mysterious possibilities of the future and prepare a horoscope he knows he'll need later." swift="Once per day, a star watcher can treat an open extract slot as though he had prepared a horoscope keyed to himself in it, choosing one formula of the appropriate level from his formula book."}

::ab[Shooting Star (Ex)]{next flavor="A star watcher can calculate trajectories with terrifying accuracy." icon=boost ability="He can apply the benefits of *studied combat,* *studied strike,* and investigator talents that add effects to *studied combat* or *studied strike* while using a starknife as a thrown weapon."}

::ab[Returning Star (Ex)]{next prereq="*Shooting Star* talent" icon=power ability="Whenever the star watcher uses a starknife as a thrown weapon against a target of his *studied combat,* the starknife automatically returns as if it had the ‹magic-enh/returning› property."}

steel_hound.name: Steel Hound
description: ## Steel Hound
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/103›

Black powder and firearms are a natural extension of the alchemical experimentation that investigators use on a regular basis. Steel hounds are investigators who have taken to using firearms in place of the more mundane weapons their counterparts favor.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down note="Steel hounds are proficient with simple weapons, plus the ‹eq-weapon/rapier›, the ‹eq-weapon/sap›, and one type of ‹main/equipment_>firearm<s› selected at 1st level. They are proficient in light armor, but not shields." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Packing Heat (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="The steel hound gains both the ‹feat/Amateur Gunslinger› and ‹feat/Gunsmithing› feats as bonus feats. He also gains a battered gun identical to the one gained by the ‹class/gunslinger›." replace="Poison lore"}
::ab[Investigator Talents]{icon=info ability="The steel hound can select the ‹feat/Extra Grit› or ‹feat/Rapid Reload› feats in place of an investigator talent." alter="Investigator talents"}

::ab[Shot in the Dark (Ex)]{head l=4 icon=power ability="A steel hound gains the following deed. This deed works and interacts with *grit* the same way as gunslinger deeds, but only the steel hound can use it. If the steel hound also has levels in gunslinger, he can spend grit points from that class to use this deed."}

::ab[Blind Shot (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A steel hound can spend 1 grit point to ignore all miss chances due to ‹rule/concealment› when making firearm attacks. This effect lasts until the end of his turn. This ability allows the steel hound to ignore concealment, but does not reveal or allow him to see the enemy." replace="Swift alchemy"}

::ab[Talented Shot (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="A steel hound can select a ‹ability/gunslinger deed<s› in place of an investigator talent. He can select from any deed available to a gunslinger of his investigator level - 4."}

tekritanin_arbiter.name: Tekritanin Arbiter
description: ## Tekritanin Arbiter
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/33›

The Tekritanin League facilitated trade and settled disputes between a vast array of diverse cultures. The arbiters were the first line of diplomatic defense, solving problems before they could become disruptive.

::ab[Tekritanin (Ex)]{icon=power-boost ability="A Tekritanin arbiter gains Tekritanin as a bonus language. Tekritanin is a fluid and evolving hybrid tongue which shares roots with many modern languages. When the arbiter speaks Tekritanin, any creature that speaks a common Inner Sea human language will understand him well enough to allow the arbiter to use the Diplomacy skill or affect the creature with language-dependent effects. At the GM's discretion, some human languages might be too divergent or unrelated to Tekritanin to use this ability immediately. In this case, the arbiter must interact with creatures speaking the language for at least 1 hour to permanently apply this ability to the foreign language." order=ap passive="A Tekritanin arbiter adds 1/2 his level (minimum 1) to Linguistics checks." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Expert Mediator (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power flavor="A Tekritanin arbiter learns to forge lasting harmony between enemies." ability="Whenever the arbiter uses the Diplomacy skill to improve the starting attitude of a creature, that attitude shift lasts for 24 hours instead of 1d4 hours, barring any circumstance or event that causes it to be decreased. If the arbiter spends 1 minute continuously interacting with a creature, he can attempt a Sense Motive check (DC = 10 + the creature's Bluff skill bonus) to identify the creature's starting attitude (per the Diplomacy skill) in regard to all other creatures present (including himself). With this understanding, the arbiter can use the Diplomacy skill to improve a target's attitude toward any other creature present. This functions as if using the Diplomacy skill to improve the target's attitude toward the arbiter, but instead uses the Diplomacy DC determined by the target's starting attitude toward the creature of the arbiter's choosing." replace="Poison lore"}

::ab[Hidden Meaning (Ex)]{flavor="A Tekritanin arbiter's deep knowledge of language provides him protection from effects that require communication." l=2 icon=def passive="He gains a +2 bonus on all saving throws against language-dependent effects." imp5="This bonus increases to +4." imp8="The bonus becomes +6." imp11="He gains immunity to language-dependent effects." replace="Poison resistance"}

::ab[Fluent Speaker (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost ability="A Tekritanin arbiter learns to speak one language with the skill of a native speaker." choice="He chooses one language he knows and gains a bonus on Disguise checks to appear as an individual native to any region where the language is primarily spoken. This bonus is equal to ‹misc/one-third› of his investigator level." passive="In addition, he gains the same bonus on both Diplomacy checks to change initial attitudes and Sense Motive checks to detect falsehoods when dealing with individuals with the selected language as their native language." order=acp special="Whenever his bonus increases (at 6th level and every 3 levels thereafter), the Tekritanin arbiter can select one additional language that his bonuses apply to, up to a maximum of +6 each for six languages at 18th level." replace="Trap sense"}

toxin_codexer.name: Toxin Codexer
description: ## Toxin Codexer
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/14›

While most investigators are well versed in the knowledge of poisons, the toxin codexer has made it a way of life. He meticulously seeks out new poisons and catalogs their effects, both those that are harmful and those that are potentially beneficial. A toxin codexer's detailed understanding of how his toxins work allows him to blend them with exact precision, creating even more deadly venoms or providing painful medicines for those in need.

::ab[Poison Lore (Ex)]{icon=power l=1 ability="A toxin codexer gains the *poison lore* ability at 1st level. This ability is otherwise identical to the investigator's *poison lore* class feature." alter="Poison lore"}
:::ab{title="Synthetic Venom (Ex)" icon=power action=Ability alter="Alchemy and replaces~trapfinding~investigator talent gained at 3rd level"}
When a toxin codexer prepares his extracts, he can use his slots to prepare short-lived poisons instead of extracts. The following poisons can be prepared into extract slots:
>::list[1st]{hl link=eq-poison all="arsenic~giant wasp poison~medium spider venom"}
>::list[2nd]{hl link=eq-poison all="black adder venom~id moss~striped toadstool"}
>::list[3rd]{hl link=eq-poison all="blue whinnis~lich dust~sassone leaf residue"}
>::list[4th]{hl link=eq-poison all="dark reaver powder~purple worm poison~third eye"}
>::list[5th]{hl link=eq-poison all="deathblade~hemlock~witch hunter's sword"}
>::list[6th]{hl link=eq-poison all="black lotus extract~dragon bile~king's sleep"}
Additional poison selections can be added to this list with GM approval.

The Fortitude save DC of these poisons is 10 + the toxin codexer's Intelligence modifier + the extract level of the poison. Poisons created in this way are unstable and become inert after 1 day if not used. Once the poison is administered to a target, the duration is as normal for that poison's type, even if longer than 1 day. These poisons can be applied to weapons (if applicable) only by the toxin codexer, unless he has the ‹discovery/infusion› alchemist discovery.
:::ab{title="Modify Toxin (Ex)" l=3 icon=def-lower-aid action=Ability replace="Keen recollection~trap sense"}

A toxin codexer learns to unlock both the beneficial and detrimental side effects of his poisons. These effects apply even if the target succeeds at her save against the poison, but they still count as poison effects. A modified toxin becomes inert if not used within 1 hour.

As a @HL[move action], the toxin codexer can expend one use of *inspiration* to apply one of the following effects to a poison in his possession, choosing either the benefit or the detriment when an effect has both options. These effects last for a number of rounds equal to the investigator's Intelligence modifier unless otherwise stated.
- Grant a +2 alchemical bonus or impose a -2 alchemical penalty on saving throws against ‹misc/exhaustion›, ‹misc/fatigue›, and sleep effects.
- Grant a +4 alchemical bonus or impose a -4 alchemical penalty on saving throws against effects with the pain descriptor.
At 6th level, the toxin codexer can also choose from the following effects:
- Affect a target so the first time she takes damage each round, she takes an additional 1d6 points of nonlethal damage. This is a pain effect.
- Inflict or remove the fatigued condition.
At 9th level, the toxin codexer can also choose from the following effects:
- Deal 1 point of ‹rule/bleed› damage or cure any ongoing bleed effects.
- Cure one disease or poison whose save DC does not exceed that of the modified poison. This has no effect on magical curses, diseases, or poisons.
At 12th level, the toxin codexer can also choose from the following effects:
- Cure one magical disease or poison whose save DC does not exceed that of the modified poison.
- Bestow a 30% spell failure chance on a target.
